sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, including a heavy 4-0 defeat to Elche. Getafe, on the other hand, are in poor form, losing 4 of their last 5 matches, conceding heavily with a 4-0 loss to Real Betis most recently. This suggests Rayo might have a slight edge going into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Rayo Vallecano have the upper hand, securing a 1-0 win and a 0-0 draw against Getafe. Historically, Rayo have not lost to Getafe in their last 9 meetings, with recent games often low-scoring and tight.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano (15th, 18 points) struggle to score (13 goals, 18th in league) but have decent possession (54.5%, 4th) and create big chances (43, 5th). Getafe (11th, 20 points) also lack firepower (13 goals) and have defensive frailties, conceding 22 goals this season. Both teams are evenly matched but prone to underperforming in attack.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Rayo’s Jorge de Frutos (4 goals) could be crucial in breaking Getafe’s defense, while Getafe’s Luis Milla (6 assists, 2nd in league) remains a creative threat despite their form. Getafe miss key forward Borja Mayoral (4 goals), which could hamper their attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Rayo Vallecano’s historical dominance over Getafe and the latter’s current losing streak, a narrow home win seems likely, aligning with Sofascore’s 40% probability for Rayo. Expect a low-scoring game, consistent with head-to-head trends (under 2.5 goals in 7/7 recent matches), and a high likelihood of cards (over 4.5 in 7/8 past meetings).
